--- iter 4 bash --- $ cat <<'EOF' STACK: Apache/2.4.67 (Debian) fronting likely Python/Flask/Werkzeug app (Flask-style 404 body, text/html utf-8); static Bootstrap under /static; unknown DB/session backend. SURFACE: GET / login page with POST form to / field username; /static/css/bootstrap.min.css asset; visible comment says testing account test:test though form only asks username; common paths /robots.txt /api /admin /debug /healthz /login(.php) /search(.php) /profile(.php) /users /user /sitemap.xml /.git/HEAD /backup all 404. BUG-HYP: IDOR after authentication: username-only login may set session for arbitrary username and authenticated pages may expose object IDs (evidence: CTF category idor; landing form has only username field; TODO test account comment; no password input despite test:test comment). WHY: Root page HTML contains POST login with only name=username and comment about test account; 404s are Flask-like behind Apache; category is idor so likely broken authorization on user/trade/account IDs after login. BREADTH VECTORS RANKED: 1 auth/login enumeration/session fixation; 2 authenticated HTML links/JS hidden routes; 3 IDOR on numeric/user id params in portfolio/orders/transactions; 4 direct API routes fuzzing; 5 static/source/backup leaks; 6 SQLi/blind in login or state-changing forms; 7 path traversal/LFI/source disclosure; 8 header/proxy path confusion. PLAN: - HYPOTHESIS: Login as test reveals authenticated routes with numeric object IDs vulnerable to IDOR (root POST has only username, category idor). - ATTACK CHAIN: POST username=test -> harvest authenticated links/JS -> identify account/transaction/profile ID parameter -> iterate IDs/users -> retrieve admin/flag-bearing object -> flag. - FIRST PROBE: curl -is -c c.txt -b c.txt -X POST -d 'username=test' http://127.0.0.1:9100/ - ABANDON IF: POST does not set a session or all authenticated routes lack object/user identifiers after full harvest/fuzz. EOF curl -is -c /tmp/cjar9100 -b /tmp/cjar9100 -X POST -d 'username=test' http://127.0.0.1:9100/ | sed -n '1,200p' STACK: Apache/2.4.67 (Debian) fronting likely Python/Flask/Werkzeug app (Flask-style 404 body, text/html utf-8); static Bootstrap under /static; unknown DB/session backend.
